statement: L3 should be treated as valid for baseline use only when hard invariants pass: non-expansion, quantity/date retention, central-actor retention, and structural parity.
rationale: Prompt intent alone is insufficient; validity requires measurable guarantees under realistic article types.
keyAssumptions: Automated checks can detect material violations with acceptable false positives.
Invariant pass rates generalize from canaries to larger runs.
validationCriteria: L3 <= L2 for >=99% and no critical violations on causal actor/date retention in gate set.
No duplicate-apply transport artifacts in stage telemetry.
critiquePoints: Rule-based checks may miss subtle semantic drift without manual adjudication.
questions: What threshold of non-critical misses is acceptable for pilot descriptive claims?
